newsSinceJun6: No material news strictly since June 6. Most recent material items sit just before the window: June 2 Q3 FY26 record results (sales $995.1M +5.8%, adj EPS $1.06 +7.1%, record 16.6% adj op margin, organic sales guide raised to 3-5%, FY26 organic adj EPS $3.94-$4.01) and the June 4 William Blair Growth Stock Conference, where management confirmed actual liquid-cooling design wins with key data-center OEMs/channel partners and 20%+ growth in the disk-drive filtration business tied to data-center demand. Facet Filtration acquisition ($828.7M) closed May 4 and is now in the guide. Today's +1.26% move is drift, not event-driven.

sources.label: StockTitan - Donaldson posts record Q3 2026 sales and earnings
Kavout - What Did Donaldson Highlight at the William Blair Conference
Investing.com - Donaldson at William Blair Conference: Strategic Growth and Resilience
Businesswire - Donaldson Completes Acquisition of Facet Filtration

verdicts.rankRationale: The latent pathway just got validated by management's own words: the June 2 print and June 4 William Blair remarks confirm actual liquid-cooling wins with key data-center OEMs plus 20%+ disk-drive filtration growth on data-center demand - the thesis moved from 'could extend into coolant cleanliness' to 'is extending'. With the stock still -5.4% YTD and uncrowded at $9.8B, none of that confirmation is in the price, and yesterday's hold rationale shows no sign of having absorbed it.
verdicts.bull: Razor-and-blade consumables (66% of revenue) plus confirmed liquid-cooling design wins give DCI a quiet, recurring-revenue toll on data-center thermal hygiene; record 16.6% margins and a raised organic guide while the stock is down YTD is the textbook latent-node setup.
verdicts.neutral: Data-center liquid cooling and disk-drive filtration are still small slices of a ~$3.9B mostly mobile/industrial filtration business; management itself flags the liquid-cooling market as fragmented with no standards, so the AI revenue line stays immaterial near-term.
verdicts.bear: Core engine/ag end-markets remain cyclical and the Facet deal is ~$0.03 EPS dilutive this year; if the AI-coolant opportunity stays niche, DCI is just a fairly valued industrial at ~21x with no AI optionality priced in either direction.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Quantified disclosure of data-center liquid-cooling and disk-drive filtration revenue or a named hyperscaler/OEM win at Q4 FY26 results (late August 2026).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Liquid-cooling filtration remains a fragmented, standards-free niche where in-house or commodity solutions win, leaving the AI extension thesis as permanent optionality that never converts.
